steps.title: Identify the clause and the interrupting phrase
steps.content: The left side (“Tales in”) begins an independent clause. The right side (“the other hand, often begin...”) continues that clause, with a parenthetical phrase interrupting in the middle.

steps.title: Apply the rule for punctuating mid-sentence phrases
steps.content:
A transitional phrase inserted in the middle of an independent clause must be set off by matching punctuation marks on both sides—typically commas.

Choice C correctly uses “Tales in English, on the other hand, often begin...” with commas on both sides of the phrase.

Options A, B, and D violate this rule by using asymmetrical or incorrect punctuation (dash and comma in A; sentence fragment in B; misused colon in D).

This matches choice C — C is correct.
